subject: refactor(pwa): harden PWA_SYSTEM — singleton, suppression, visibility, typed check
body:
Align usePWAUpdate with glow-props PWA_SYSTEM pattern:

- Extract SW lifecycle state into module-level singleton (src/lib/pwa.ts)
  so registration, hasUpdate, and offlineReady survive AppLayout remounts
  instead of re-firing "update available" banners after route changes.
- Thin React wrapper uses useSyncExternalStore for tearing-safe sync.
- 30-second wasJustUpdated() suppression via sessionStorage — onNeedRefresh
  skips while the post-reload SW lifecycle settles.
- visibilitychange listener triggers registration.update() when the tab
  regains focus (catches updates faster than the 60min poll, esp. on iOS).
- controllerchange reload guard registered once; auto-reloads only when
  _userClickedUpdate is set so background tab lifecycle events don't yank
  the page out from under the user.
- checkForUpdate(): Promise<'no-sw'|'done'|'error'> for toast feedback.
- onRegisterError routes to debugLog for DebugPill surfacing.
- Manifest: id, scope, prefer_related_applications (stable identity +
  avoid Chrome skipping beforeinstallprompt).
- Workbox: cleanupOutdatedCaches + explicit globPatterns.

Preserves existing offlineReady + dismissOfflineReady in the hook return
since AppLayout uses the banner; swap to Toast system when it lands.

Tags: pwa, refactor, infrastructure, service-worker
Complexity: 3
Urgency: 2
Impact: user-facing
Risk: medium
Debt: paid
Epic: pwa-hardening
Semver: patch
